stimulus:
The following text is from a 2002 novel by a Caribbean American author. The eight-year-old narrator lives with her family in Trinidad. Tía Beatriz is the narrator's aunt.

Tía Beatriz, who was the eldest of my mother's sisters, had moved to Canada five years before, when I was only three, and I could not picture her face at all. I climbed onto the chair by the hall cabinet and pulled out the old album of family photographs. But every picture was from long ago, taken when Tía Beatriz was still a teenager. However hard I tried, I could not put together what she might look like today. My imagination, though, rushed in to fill the empty space.
stem: According to the text, why does the narrator pull out the old album of family photographs?

answerOptions.content: She wants to remind her aunt of an event captured in one of the pictures.
She hopes the photographs will help her recall what her aunt looked like.
She is curious about the clothes her mother wore as a teenager.
She wants to use the photographs as inspiration for a school assignment.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The text says the narrator cannot picture her aunt's face and pulls out the album in an attempt to put together what she might look like.

Choice A is incorrect; the text does not mention any event the narrator wants her aunt to remember. Choice C is incorrect; the text does not discuss what the narrator's mother wore. Choice D is incorrect; the text does not mention a school assignment.
